reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding the fips=1 kernel boot parameter, which is only supported at installation time. Changing this on an existing RHEL 9 system can prevent the system from booting and may lock out administrators. The finding description explicitly states the fix is not supported on a pre‑existing system, making remediation high risk.
estimated_impact: reboot required; Potential boot failure; Loss of SSH/console access; System instability due to unsupported FIPS enablement; Non‑compliance if not properly validated
